questionText: Provide details on your organization's chemical products.
questionKo: details on 귀사의 chemical products.을/를 입력하십시오.
simpleHelp: Unlike most other high-impact sectors identified by CDP, the chemicals sector is heterogeneous and highly diverse in structure, and can even be characterized as a group of disparate subsectors. It is, therefore, problematic to consider sector-wide intensities. However, it is useful to identify the most important chemical production processes from an environmental or transition risk perspective and shed light on the presence and impact of them within and between organizations.

columns.text: Direct emissions intensity (metric tons CO2e per metric ton of product)
columns.textKo: Direct 배출집약도 (미터톤 CO2e per metric ton of product)

columns.text: Electricity intensity (MWh per metric ton of product)
columns.textKo: Electricity intensity (MWh per metric ton of product)

columns.text: Steam intensity (MWh per metric ton of product)
columns.textKo: Steam intensity (MWh per metric ton of product)

columns.text: Steam/ heat recovered (MWh per metric ton of product)
columns.textKo: Steam/ heat recovered (MWh per metric ton of product)

guidance.text:
General 
Complete the table for each chemical output product selected in column 1. 
Your production, capacity, and intensity figures should be for the reporting year only (as defined by your answer to 1.4). 
If you did not have any capacity, production or intensity in the reporting year then enter 0 (zero) in the relevant field. 
Negative numbers are not allowed. 
You should apply the same logic to your boundary definition as provided in question 7.19. 
Output product (column 1) 
Select the product(s) relevant to your organization's chemical-related activities. Select as many as applicable. You may also wish to select those with no production in the reporting year to confirm zero activity. 
If you select "Other (Please specify)," provide a label for the output product. 
Production (metric tons) (column 2) 
Enter the production in metric tons, for the product selected in column 1. 
Capacity (metric tons) (column 3) 
Enter the production capacity in metric tons of the output product selected in column 1. 
Direct emissions intensity (metric tons CO2e per metric ton of product) (column 4) 
Report the direct emissions intensity, in metric tons CO2e per metric ton of chemical product, in the reporting year, for the product selected in column 1. 
Direct emissions include emissions from the use of fuel for process heating and feedstock related emissions (process emissions). 
Electricity intensity (MWh per metric ton of product) (column 5) 
Report the electric intensity, in MWh per metric ton of chemical product, in the reporting year, for the product selected in column 1. 
Because this question relates to the process-level, Scope 1 and Scope 2 terminology is not used here. No distinction needs to be made on whether the electricity is sourced from inside or outside of the organizational boundary. 
Scope 2 emissions are not requested to avoid the influence of emission factors that are unrelated to the process. 
Steam intensity (MWh per metric ton of product) (column 6) 
Report the steam intensity, in MWh per metric ton of chemical product, in the reporting year, for the product selected in column 1. 
Steam/heat recovered (MWh per metric ton of product) (column 7) 
Report the steam/heat recovered, in MWh per metric ton of chemical product, in the reporting year, for the product selected in column 1. 
Many processes in the chemicals sector are exothermic. You should enter here the MWh of steam/heat that is recovered from the process. 
Comment (column 8) (optional) 
Use this column to provide any additional information relevant to the chemical product selected in column 1.
guidance.textKo: 일반 Complete the table for each chemical output product 선택 in 열 1. Your production, capacity, 및 intensity figures 해야 합니다 be 에 대한 보고연도 only (as defined by your answer to 1.4). 만약 you did not have 하나 이상 capacity, production 또는 intensity in the 보고연도 then enter 0 (zero) in the 관련 field. Negative numbers are not allowed. You 해야 합니다 apply the same logic to your boundary definition as 입력 in 문항 7.19. Output product (열 1) Select the product(s) 관련 to 귀사의 chemical-related activities. Select as many as 해당되는 경우. You may also wish to select those with no production in the 보고연도 to confirm zero activity. 만약 you select "Other (직접 입력)," provide a label 에 대한 output product. Production (metric tons) (열 2) Enter the production in metric tons, 에 대한 product 선택 in 열 1. Capacity (metric tons) (열 3) Enter the production capacity in metric tons 의 output product 선택 in 열 1. Direct 배출집약도 (미터톤 CO2e per metric ton of product) (열 4) Report the direct 배출집약도, in 미터톤 CO2e per metric ton of chemical product, in the 보고연도, 에 대한 product 선택 in 열 1. Direct emissions include emissions from the use of fuel for 프로세스 heating 및 feedstock related emissions (프로세스 emissions). Electricity intensity (MWh per metric ton of product) (열 5) Report the electric intensity, in MWh per metric ton of chemical product, in the 보고연도, 에 대한 product 선택 in 열 1. Because this 문항 relates to the 프로세스-level, Scope 1 및 Scope 2 terminology is not used here. No distinction needs to be made on whether the electricity is sourced from inside 또는 outside 의 조직경계. Scope 2 배출량 are not requested to avoid the influence of emission factors that are unrelated to the 프로세스. Steam intensity (MWh per metric ton of product) (열 6) Report the steam intensity, in MWh per metric ton of chemical product, in the 보고연도, 에 대한 product 선택 in 열 1. Steam/heat recovered (MWh per metric ton of product) (열 7) Report the steam/heat recovered, in MWh per metric ton of chemical product, in the 보고연도, 에 대한 product 선택 in 열 1. Many 프로세스es in the chemicals sector are exothermic. You 해야 합니다 enter here the MWh of steam/heat that is recovered from the 프로세스. Comment (열 8) (optional) Use this 열 to provide 하나 이상 additional information 관련 to the chemical product 선택 in 열 1.

guidance.text:
High Value Chemicals (Steam cracking): High value chemicals (HVCs) produced via steam cracking include ethylene, propylene from the pyrolysis gas of steam crackers, benzene (contained amounts, excluding extracted amounts), butadiene (also contained), acetylene, and hydrogen sold (as fuel).
Steam cracking: Steam cracking is the main method of breaking down large molecules of hydrocarbons, in which a gaseous or liquid hydrocarbon is diluted with steam and then heated. The main product for steam cracking process is HVCs.
guidance.textKo: High Value Chemicals (Steam cracking): High value chemicals (HVCs) produced via steam cracking include ethylene, propylene from the pyrolysis gas of steam crackers, benzene (contained amounts, 제외 extracted amounts), butadiene (also contained), acetylene, 및 hydrogen sold (as fuel). Steam cracking: Steam cracking is the main method of breaking down large molecules of hydrocarbons, in which a gaseous 또는 liquid hydrocarbon is diluted with steam 및 then heated. The main product for steam cracking 프로세스 is HVCs.

criteria.criteria:
Points will be awarded per completed cell in proportion to the number of rows disclosed. Partially completed rows will not receive full points.
A maximum of 7/7 points is available for this question.
criteria.criteriaKo: 점수가 부여됩니다 per 완성된 칸(셀) in proportion to the number of 행 공개. 부분적으로 완성된 행은 최고점을 받을 수 없습니다. 이 문항에서 획득 가능한 최고점은 7/7점입니다.

criteria.criteria:
Table completed - 2 points
A maximum of 2/2 points is available for this question.
criteria.criteriaKo: Table 완성 - 2 점 이 문항에서 획득 가능한 최고점은 2/2점입니다.

simpleHelpKo: Unlike most other high-impact sectors identified by CDP, the chemicals sector is heterogeneous 및 highly diverse in structure, 및 can even be characterized as a group of disparate subsectors. It is, therefore, problematic to consider sector-wide intensities. However, it is useful to identify the most important chemical production 프로세스es from an environmental 또는 transition risk perspective 및 shed light on the presence 및 impact 의m within 및 between organizations.
